steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Winnemucca creates suspense by emphasizing her physical response to an event. The correct quote must show (1) a tense or threatening event and (2) a bodily reaction — racing heart, trembling, breathlessness — that builds tension.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"Oh, how my heart jumped...I thought whoever it was would hear my heart beat."': A horse approaches in the dark; Winnemucca's pounding heart is described twice, directly conveying physical fear and building suspense. ✓
  • Choice B — '"Oh, how happy everybody was! One could hear laughter everywhere..."': This is joyful celebration, not suspense, and describes no physical distress. ✗
  • Choice C — '"Late in that fall, there came news that my grandfather was on his way home..."': A factual account of events with no physical response and no tension. ✗
  • Choice D — '"That same fall, after my grandfather came home, he told my father to take charge..."': Narrates a directive with no physical reaction or suspense. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
